description: o2r overview: service graph (agent topology), live span rate / p95 latency / error rate, recent traces, and a TraceQL cheatsheet. Default home dashboard.

options.content: **o2r-overview** is the dashboard the operator lands on. Top: live agent topology + flow-rate. Middle: per-span latency and error rate, sourced from Tempo's span-metrics generator. Bottom: TraceQL cheatsheet for opening a trace by session ID. For the per-step LUCA-flow breakdown, [open the LUCA-flow dashboard](/d/luca-flow/luca-flow).

panels.title: Agent topology (service graph)
panels.description: Edges = relay forwards. Heat = request rate. Sourced from Tempo's `traces_service_graph_request_total` metric, which is populated by the metrics-generator on every relay span.

panels.title: Span rate (last 5m)
panels.description: Total spans per second across the whole stack. Useful for spotting whether a luca-demo run is actually emitting.

panels.title: Error rate (last 5m)
panels.description: Fraction of spans with `status=error` over total. Lower is better. The luca-flow demo deliberately produces ~12.5% errors (worker-d crash + worker-g topology violation), so green here = we are catching the failure modes.

panels.title: p95 span latency by name
panels.description: Histogram quantile of span durations from `traces_spanmetrics_latency_bucket`. Click an exemplar dot (orange diamonds) to drill into the trace that produced it.

panels.title: Span call rate by agent role
panels.description: Per-role span throughput. `relay` and `orchestrator` should always dominate; spikes in `worker` correlate with luca-flow runs.

panels.title: Recent error traces
panels.description: Live TraceQL search for any erroring trace. Click a row to open the full waterfall + tree.

options.content:
Open Tempo Explore -> paste any of these. Detailed grammar at the [TraceQL docs](https://grafana.com/docs/tempo/latest/traceql/).

```traceql
{ trace:duration > 2s }                                    # slow traces
{ status = error }                                          # error traces
{ resource.service.name = "luca-relay" && status = error }  # relay errors
{ span.session.id = "luca-aurora-XXXX" }                    # one session
{ span.a2a.method = "message/send" }                        # A2A sends
{ span.agent.role = "worker" } >> { span.agent.role = "validator" }   # worker -> validator
{ name = "orchestrator.acceptance" && span.o2r.step.acceptance.decision = "crashed" }
```

### TraceQL metrics (requires `local-blocks` flush, on by default in this stack)

```traceql
{ resource.service.name = "luca-relay" } | rate() by (span.agent.role)
{ } | quantile_over_time(span:duration, .95) by (resource.service.name)
```

### From a span attribute, on Tempo Explore -> Service Graph tab

- Group by `agent_role` to see relay vs worker hot paths.
- Group by `session_id` to see one luca-flow run as a graph.
- Group by `a2a_method` to see message/send vs message/stream usage.

title: o2r overview
